```{=html}
<aside class="side-panel glossary-sidebar">
<details open>
<summary>Glossary</summary>
<dl>
  <dt><code>S<sub>t</sub></code>, <code>P</code></dt>
  <dd>Token spot price (USD/kVCM) and protocol constant (kVCM / tonne retired).
  Per-tonne USD protocol price is <code>π<sub>t</sub> = P · S<sub>t</sub></code>.</dd>

  <dt><code>I<sub>T</sub> = ∫₀<sup>T</sup> S<sub>t</sub> dt</code></dt>
  <dd>Area under the price curve. The shared random kernel: fee revenue is
  linear in <code>I<sub>T</sub></code>, back-to-back P&amp;L is affine in
  <code>I<sub>T</sub></code>.</dd>

  <dt>Coverage <code>τ<sub>cov</sub></code></dt>
  <dd>Fraction of the horizon pre-bought inventory lasts,
  <code>min(1, k / (N·P))</code>. The Model calls the same dial
  <code>α</code>; the Simulator derives it from <code>(k, C<sub>basis</sub>)</code>.</dd>

  <dt>Stopping time <code>τ</code> (switching)</dt>
  <dd>First time <code>S<sub>t</sub></code> crosses the upper barrier
  <code>H = h · S<sub>0</sub></code>, truncated at <code>T</code>. Distinct from
  the coverage fraction above: this one carries units of time.</dd>

  <dt>VaR<sub>95</sub> / CVaR<sub>95</sub></dt>
  <dd>Loss threshold at the 5% quantile of <code>−Π</code> / mean loss inside
  that 5% tail. CVaR is the informative figure once a tail is fat or skewed.
  On the fee book these are "low end of revenue", not loss.</dd>

  <dt>Fee book / principal book</dt>
  <dd>Fee: markup <code>f · π<sub>t</sub></code> per tonne, no inventory.
  Principal: fixed quote <code>Q</code>, five variants — Matched (§3a),
  Back-to-back (§3b), Partial (§3c), Syndicated (§3d), Switching (§3e).</dd>
</dl>
</details>
</aside>
```
